attributes.description: Alerts when peak (maximum) target response time exceeds a tunable threshold. Serves as a tail-latency proxy where CloudWatch percentiles are unavailable.
attributes.name: [AWS ELB OTel] High Application Load Balancer target response time (tail)

investigation_guide.blob:
## High Target Response Time (Tail)

Peak `TargetResponseTime` (`Maximum` stat) captures the worst single observation in the window — a proxy for p95/p99 tail latency since percentile statistics are not collected by this source.

### Triage Steps
1. Identify the affected load balancer, target group, and region.
2. Look for outlier requests: slow queries, cold starts, GC pauses, or timeout retries.
3. Compare with the average response time alert — a high tail with normal average suggests a few bad requests; both elevated suggests systemic slowness.
4. Check target health and whether remaining healthy targets are overloaded.

### Tuning
- Adjust the `max_response_time > 5.0` threshold (seconds) to approximate your p99 SLO.
- Pair with the average response time alert for typical vs tail latency visibility.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.elb.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/ApplicationELB"
    AND MetricName == "TargetResponseTime"
    AND stat == "Maximum"
| STATS max_response_time = MAX(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ApplicationELB/TargetResponseTime`)
    BY LoadBalancer, TargetGroup, cloud.region
// Tail latency threshold in seconds — tune to approximate p99 SLO (default 5.0s)
| WHERE max_response_time > 5.0
| SORT max_response_time DESC
